result:
Build is clean, no errors. No temporary instrumentation was added.

**Root cause:** `str_format` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1569` was a Phase-A stub. The `%d`/`%i` arm (line 1631) was tagged `TODO(port): width/precision/flags from spec not applied` and just did `format!("{}", n)`, so `string.format("%010d", 23)` returned `"23"` instead of `"0000000023"`. The `%s`, `%c`, `%u`, `%o`, `%x`, `%X`, `%f`, `%e`, `%E`, `%g`, `%G` arms had the same problem.

**Fix:** Added a `FmtSpec` struct + `parse_fmt_spec` parser for printf-style flags (`-+#0 ` plus width/precision), and two helpers `pad_str` (string-style: precision truncates, pad with spaces) and `pad_int` (numeric-style: precision sets min digits, `0` flag zero-pads, sign prefix kept outside zero pad). Wired every numeric/string/char arm through these. Also added `format_float`/`format_exp`/`format_g` for proper `%f`/`%e`/`%g` width-and-precision behavior matching C semantics.

**New state:** Verified directly — `%010d` → `0000000023`, `%-50s` of `"a"` is 50 chars, `%.20s` truncates to 20, `%20s` right-aligns, `%f` of 10.3 → `10.300000`. The line-210 assertion that was failing now passes. The mega-loop will re-test for any remaining failures further into strings.lua.

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5; echo "exit=$?"
